recipes.name: Vegetarian Thai Green Curry With Tofu Recipe
recipes.ingredients: Tofu, green curry paste, coconut milk, bell peppers, bamboo shoots, zucchini, red curry paste, fish sauce, palm sugar, kaffir lime leaves, cilantro
recipes.time: 1 hour total (60 minutes cooking + 10 minutes prep)
recipes.reason: Quick to prepare and cook, perfect for a quick vegetarian meal.

tips.name: Vegetarian Thai Green Curry With Tofu Recipe
tips: Reduce the amount of red curry paste to decrease sodium and sugar content.
Increase the portion of vegetables and reduce coconut milk for lower fat content.
Use low-sodium fish sauce or tamari as a healthier alternative.

instructions: Peel and finely chop the garlic cloves.
Finely dice the ginger root.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of ghee over medium heat.
Add the chopped garlic and ginger to the pan.
Sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the chopped onions and sauté until they turn golden brown and soft.
Add the sliced tomatoes and cook until they break down and form a smooth puree.

recipes.name: Quinoa and Vegetable Saute Recipe
recipes.ingredients: quinoa, mixed vegetables (like bell peppers, zucchini), onion, garlic, olive oil, salt, pepper
recipes.reason: High protein content from quinoa, quick to prepare, balanced meal

recipes.name: High Protein Broccoli and Dill Stuffed Paratha Recipe
recipes.ingredients: whole wheat flour, water, oil for frying, broccoli, paneer (or tofu), dill, spices
recipes.reason: Stuffed parathas with high protein broccoli filling, nutritious and satisfying

recipes.name: Tofu and Chickpea Patty Recipe (Cutlets)
recipes.ingredients: tofu, chickpeas, breadcrumbs, spices, vegetables (optional), oil for frying
recipes.reason: Combines high protein sources of tofu and chickpeas in a quick-to-prepare snack

tips.name: Quinoa and Vegetable Saute Recipe
tips: Increase the vegetable variety to ensure a wide range of nutrients.
Use less oil or opt for cooking in water to reduce fat content.
Add herbs like parsley or cilantro for additional flavor without extra calories.

tips.name: High Protein Broccoli and Dill Stuffed Paratha Recipe
tips: Use whole wheat flour in parathas to keep the carb count at a healthy level.
Reduce oil content by using non-stick cooking methods or baking instead of frying.
Add some grated carrots or sweet potatoes for extra fiber without increasing calories.

tips.name: Tofu and Chickpea Patty Recipe (Cutlets)
tips: Replace breadcrumbs with finely ground oats to boost the fiber content.
Use vegetable broth instead of water for cooking to add flavor without fat.
Experiment with different spices like turmeric or cumin to enhance the taste naturally.

instructions: Pre-soak chickpeas in water for at least 6 hours.
Cook chickpeas in a pressure cooker or on the stovetop until soft.
Drain and rinse cooked chickpeas.
Add drained chickpeas to a large mixing bowl and mash with a potato masher or fork.
Incorporate remaining ingredients and mix well, using your hands to ensure thorough combining.
Divide mixture into 10-12 portions and shape into oval or round patties.
Slightly flatten the patty dough in your palm.
Preheat a skillet and add a thin layer of oil.
Cook patties on low to medium heat for several minutes, flipping occasionally until golden brown.
Rinse 2 cups quinoa and cook with 4 cups water and ½ teaspoon salt in a rice cooker or covered saucepan for about 15 minutes.
Fluff cooked quinoa with a fork and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and cook until they crackle.
Add ginger and sauté for a few minutes, then add peas and carrots. Cook for another few minutes.
Add chopped onions and pepper, sautéing until softened.
Stir in spice powders and salt; let infuse for a moment before adding cooked quinoa.
Gently toss all ingredients together and season with additional salt as needed.
Garnish with fresh herbs and olive oil. Serve hot.
Mix flour, water, and other dough ingredients to form a firm, non-sticky dough; cover and rest.
Heat a saucepan, add cumin seeds and asafetida, and sauté until fragrant.
Add chopped vegetables and cook until tender.
In another bowl, mix red chilli powder, salt, besan flour, and water to create a paste.
Combine the vegetable mixture with the besan paste and cook until well combined and fully cooked.
Allow the filling to cool slightly.
Divide dough into small balls and roll each into a 3-inch circle.
Place a tablespoon of cooled stuffing in the center of each circle, fold over to form dumplings, seal edges, and flatten.
Preheat a skillet on medium heat; place parathas, coating lightly with oil or ghee.
Cook until golden brown spots appear on both sides, flipping occasionally.
Serve hot.

recipes.name: High Protein Spinach & Soy Bites Recipe
recipes.ingredients: Soy flour, spinach, chickpea flour, spices
recipes.reason: Quick to prepare and high in protein. Great as a snack or meal.

recipes.name: Kabuli Chana Chaat - High Protein Snack of Chickpea Chaat
recipes.ingredients: Chickpeas, tamarind chutney, chaat masala, onions, tomatoes
recipes.reason: Chickpeas are high in protein and this snack is crunchy with various flavors.

recipes.name: Vegan Soya Aloo Curry Recipe
recipes.reason: Hearty and filling. Uses soya chunks which are high in protein.

tips.name: High Protein Spinach & Soy Bites Recipe
tips: Consider reducing the amount of chickpea flour to lower carbohydrate intake.
Incorporate more leafy greens for additional nutrients without increasing calories.

tips.name: Kabuli Chana Chaat - High Protein Snack of Chickpea Chaat
tips: Limit the tamarind chutney to manage added sugars.
Increase the portion size of vegetables for more fiber and volume.

tips.name: Vegan Soya Aloo Curry Recipe
tips: Increase the ratio of soya chunks to potatoes to boost protein content.
Use less oil during cooking to reduce fat intake.

instructions: Boil water in a pan, add tomatoes, let sit for a few minutes, then remove the skin and finely chop.
Heat oil in a kadhai (wok) over medium heat.
Add asafoetida powder and cumin seeds, fry until fragrant.
Add grated ginger, chopped garlic, and onions, sauté until onions become translucent.
Add potatoes, spice powders, and salt, cook for a few minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes and slit green chillies, cook for 2-3 more minutes.
Stir in soya chunks, cook for a few minutes, adding water as needed.
Serve with whole wheat lachha paratha or achari masala puri.
Dry roast soya flakes in a skillet over medium heat for three minutes.
Allow the roasted flakes to cool, then grind them to a coarse powder in a mixer.
Transfer the ground soya flakes to a mixing bowl.
In a large bowl, combine all ingredients: ground soya flakes, almond meal, bread crumbs, paneer, red chilli flakes, salt, and pepper.
Mix until the dough-like mixture forms.
Divide the mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into small balls.
Preheat the oven to 200°C. Grease a baking sheet with a little oil.
Bake spinach bites in the preheated oven for about 20 minutes or until outer layer turns crisp and brown.
Alternatively, pan fry spinach bites in a paniyaram pan using a little oil.
Transfer cooked spinach & soy bites to a serving platter.
Serve as an appetizer with other dishes.
